scope.summary: The stale '=0.7.4' comments were already gone, but confirming the 0.8.4 wallet-error variant set (which the bead required before any bump) found WalletError::TooLarge unmapped: doctor reported an oversized wallet as a malformed PEM.
out_of_scope: The driver version doctor reports, which is already derived from oraclemcp_db::DRIVER_VERSION (a re-export of the driver's own VERSION const) and therefore cannot go stale by hand.
scripts/release_surface_sync_check.sh and scripts/release_preflight.sh, which mention 0.7.4 only as an illustrative example of driver/server drift, not as a claim about the current pin.

The string-based classify_wallet_error path: TooLarge is mapped through the typed wallet_error_kind path, and the driver's message ('wallet data exceeds maximum size of N bytes') is not one the string classifier's outer gate admits.

entry_points.name: wallet_error_kind (crates/oraclemcp-core/src/doctor.rs)
entry_points.trace: The doc comment claimed every variant the pinned =0.8.4 driver can produce was mapped explicitly; oracledb-protocol-0.8.4 src/tls/wallet.rs declares WalletError::TooLarge { maximum_bytes } (MAX_WALLET_FILE_BYTES = 16 MiB) and it was absent, so it hit `_ => Pem` and produced the remediation 'regenerate ewallet.pem with valid PEM certificate material' for bytes that were never parsed. TooLarge is now a distinct DoctorWalletErrorKind with its own label, remediation, and ErrorClass::InvalidArguments. wallet_error_kind_maps_every_pinned_driver_variant exhausts the driver enum WITHOUT a wildcard, so a future variant fails compilation instead of silently landing on Pem; it also pins that TooLarge is not fallthrough-eligible, matching the driver's falls_through_to_autologin (KeyDecrypt/Pkcs12/PasswordRequired/UnsupportedFormat) exactly.

limitations: Discriminating, verified by mutation: deleting the TooLarge arm fails the new test with 'mapping drifted for wallet data exceeds maximum size of 16777216 bytes — left: Pem, right: TooLarge'.

PRE-EXISTING RED, not caused by this change: cargo test -p oraclemcp-core --lib doctor:: reports 2 failures, legacy_migration_refuses_symlink_swaps_at_atomic_install and legacy_state_layout_detects_and_migrates_audit_jsonl_once. Both were introduced by commit ee1b23ad (another agent's atomic-migration hardening): its new parent-traversal refusal rejects the test helper doctor_tmp_dir, which returns a path containing literal '..'. PROVEN independent of this work by running those two tests in a clean worktree at HEAD with none of my changes present — they fail there identically. All other doctor tests pass (55 of 57), including both wallet tests.
No live Oracle wallet over 16 MiB was produced; the mapping is exercised through the driver's own typed error value, not a filesystem fixture.

known_defects.description: cargo test -p oraclemcp-core --lib doctor:: is red at HEAD from ee1b23ad: doctor_tmp_dir returns a '..'-containing path that the new parent-traversal refusal rejects, failing legacy_migration_refuses_symlink_swaps_at_atomic_install and legacy_state_layout_detects_and_migrates_audit_jsonl_once.
